title: rubus × rhamnifolius
intro: Rubus × rhamnifolius, also known as the black raspberry hybrid, is a cross between the black raspberry (Rubus occidentalis) and the thimbleberry (Rubus parviflorus), resulting in a unique plant with characteristics of both parent species. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its high antioxidant content and anti-inflammatory properties, which are thought to contribute to its potential health benefits. The health benefits of Rubus × rhamnifolius are said to include reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and potentially even inhibiting the growth of certain cancer cells. Rubus × rhamnifolius can be prepared into various herbal products, such as teas, tinctures, and jams, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and reap its health benefits.
